title: About FermatMind
summary: FermatMind is a self-understanding and growth platform that turns assessments, career exploration, and decision support into a system people can use over time.
contentMd:
## Who we are

FermatMind is a self-understanding and growth platform for students, early-career professionals, career changers, and long-term builders. We turn assessment results into clearer language for decisions, communication, learning, and career exploration.

## Why we exist

Most people are not starting from zero. They already sense their preferences, stress points, strengths, and tradeoffs, but the information is scattered. FermatMind helps convert those signals into structured hypotheses that can be discussed, tested, and reviewed over time.

## How we build

We combine measurement, interpretation, action planning, and review. A result should not stop at a label; it should help users understand patterns, test choices, and improve future decisions.

## Our boundaries

FermatMind does not provide medical diagnosis, emergency care, hiring decisions, school admission decisions, or legal advice. Our tools support self-observation and planning, and high-risk decisions should involve qualified professionals.

title: FermatMind Public-Benefit Giving Plan
summary: FermatMind's public-benefit giving plan is an independent company action recorded by FermatMind. It keeps public giving records separate from assessment logic, product use, data handling, and business boundaries. The page does not ask users to donate, and product use should not be described as a personal donation action.
metaDescription: FermatMind's public-benefit giving plan is an independent company action recorded by FermatMind. It keeps public giving records separate from assessment logic, product use, data handling, and business boundaries. The page does not ask users to donate, and product use should not be described as a personal donation action.
contentMd:
## Hero

FermatMind is turning its long-term interest in youth growth, structured decision support, and public-interest issues into a transparent company action.

Beginning June 1, 2026, FermatMind plans to make a daily public-benefit gift and publish a record of that gift on its website and public channels. The current recipient organization is the United Nations Foundation.

This page records FermatMind's own giving plan. It should be read as FermatMind's public record, not as a statement that changes the role, status, or responsibility of any outside organization.

## Why We Publish This

FermatMind is not only about a single assessment result. Our work is focused on helping young people build clearer self-understanding, career exploration, and long-term decision capacity.

The public-benefit giving plan is one way to turn that long-term responsibility into a visible record. We want this action to remain transparent, reviewable, and bounded. It should not be used to describe FermatMind as a separate legal public-benefit body, and it should not change how users understand our assessments, reports, privacy rules, or product boundaries.

This plan does not change FermatMind's assessment logic, report generation, privacy policy, or user data handling.

## Recipient Organization

Current recipient organization: United Nations Foundation.

The United Nations Foundation supports work connected to global public issues and the Sustainable Development Goals. FermatMind chose this recipient because its public mission is aligned with our long-term interest in youth growth, public-interest issues, and responsible decision support.

FermatMind decides, records, and explains this giving activity itself. Public records on this page should be read as FermatMind's own company record.

## Latest Records Placeholder

Beginning June 1, 2026, FermatMind plans to publish daily records after each public-benefit gift is completed. Records may include the date, recipient organization, gift status, proof status, and public-channel update links.

To protect payment security and personal privacy, any public proof materials will redact payment accounts, full transaction numbers, email addresses, phone numbers, addresses, and other sensitive information.

The full daily giving record system will be launched in a later version.

## Full Archive Entrypoint

The full archive entrypoint will be available after the Daily Giving Ledger is launched. A later version is planned to include a daily record index and monthly archive pages for published giving records.

Before the archive system is available, this page only provides the program explanation, boundary note, and future record entrypoint notice.

## Public Updates

After each daily gift is completed, FermatMind plans to publish a corresponding public update and link back to the website record.

The website record remains the primary reference. Public screenshots, links, or proof notes do not change the boundary of this page: the giving record belongs to FermatMind's own public explanation system.

## Important Boundary Note

The phrase "public-benefit giving plan" refers to FermatMind's own public record of company giving.

This plan does not create a separate legal body, change FermatMind's business identity, or change the responsibilities of the recipient organization.

FermatMind does not collect money from users for this page. Product use should not be described as a personal donation action.

## FAQ

### Q1: What is the FermatMind Public-Benefit Giving Plan?

It is FermatMind's own public record of company giving. Beginning June 1, 2026, FermatMind plans to make a daily public-benefit gift and publish a record on its website and public channels.

### Q2: Does this create a separate legal body?

No. This page records a company action by FermatMind. It does not create a separate legal body or change FermatMind's business identity.

### Q3: What is the role of the recipient organization?

The recipient organization is named so that records can be understood clearly. FermatMind is responsible for deciding, recording, and explaining its own giving activity.

### Q4: Are the gifts restricted to a specific designated program?

Unless a future record identifies a specific designation, this page should not be read as claiming that gifts are restricted to a specific project, region, team, or program.

### Q5: Does completing a FermatMind assessment directly trigger a gift?

No. The giving plan is operated by FermatMind. User actions should not be described as directly triggering a specific gift, and this page does not ask users to donate.

### Q6: How can the records be reviewed?

FermatMind plans to publish records on its website and public channels. The full daily giving record system will be launched in a later version. Public proof materials will redact payment and personal-sensitive information.

## Related Links

### FermatMind Pages

- [FermatMind Charter](/en/charter)
- [Brand](/en/brand)
- [Method Boundaries](/en/method-boundaries)
- [Policies](/en/policies)

### External Reference Links

- [United Nations Foundation giving page](https://x.com/FermatMind/status/2064356137080889431)

title: Work With FermatMind
summary: FermatMind is building an AI-native assessment and structured decision-support platform. We are looking for people who can connect psychometrics, product design, engineering systems, and long-term content quality.
metaDescription: FermatMind is building an AI-native assessment and structured decision-support platform. We are looking for people who can connect psychometrics, product design, engineering systems, and long-term content quality.
contentMd:
# Work With FermatMind

## The work we care about
FermatMind works on assessment interpretation, career exploration, product systems, content quality, and user-facing decision support. The work requires careful language, product judgment, engineering reliability, privacy awareness, and respect for the limits of assessment tools.

## Possible collaboration areas
Depending on future needs, relevant areas may include research and methodology, content and editing, product design, user research, frontend and backend engineering, data analysis, quality assurance, operations, and responsible growth.

## What we value in collaborators
We value clarity, careful reasoning, respect for human complexity, strong execution, and willingness to revise work when evidence changes. We also value sensitivity to privacy, psychological safety, and high-risk settings where assessment results can be misused.

## Open Roles

FermatMind is building an AI-native assessment and structured decision-support platform. We are looking for people who can connect psychometrics, product design, engineering systems, and long-term content quality. The roles below are real open directions, not a generic talent pool or broad collaboration invitation.

These roles will contribute to FermatMind’s long-term infrastructure: psychometric data, norm calibration, brand systems, product UI, Laravel 12 backend, Next.js frontend, Release Train deployment workflow, and engineering SOP.

### 1. Psychometrics Data Engineer

**Compensation**: 15K-25K RMB / month, pre-tax, with statutory social insurance and housing fund under the applicable employment arrangement  
**Work mode**: Full-time, remote supported, Shanghai-based  
**Status**: Rolling opening; the role will be updated periodically based on team needs

### Who this is for

This role is for someone with formal research experience in psychometrics, personality assessment, vocational interests, career development, organizational psychology, educational measurement, cognitive assessment, applied statistics, or quantitative psychology.

Published paper(s) in relevant fields are required. Verifiable formal research output may be submitted as supporting material, but it does not replace the paper requirement. Supporting materials may include journal papers, conference papers, preprints, empirical research in a graduate thesis, or formal project work that clearly explains methodology, data, model design, and limitations.

### What you will own

- Translate psychometric research, career-personality assessment studies, scale structures, norm calibration, and AI-assisted research workflows into usable data structures, product logic, and calibration processes.
- Support stronger norm calibration, dimension interpretation, and result boundaries around Big Five, MBTI, Enneagram, IQ, EQ, and Holland / RIASEC.
- Use compliant AI-tooling support provided by the team to turn classic psychometric papers into research notes, Prompt workflows, calibration procedures, and product logic.
- Help design mappings between norm databases, vector structures, occupation tags, personality tags, item dimensions, and explanatory text.
- Identify overpromising claims, causal misreadings, clinicalized wording, or overly strong career prediction claims in assessment explanations.

### What we expect

- Published paper(s) in relevant fields are required. Verifiable formal research output may be submitted as supporting material, but it does not replace the paper requirement.
- You understand psychometrics, scale development, personality assessment, vocational interests, or related quantitative research methods.
- You understand reliability, validity, factor analysis, norms, item quality, and model boundaries.
- You can read English academic papers and break down their methodology, samples, limitations, and applicable scenarios into executable workflows.
- You can communicate with product, engineering, and content teams without leaving academic ideas only in paper language.

### Nice to have

- Familiarity with Item Response Theory (IRT), Classical Test Theory (CTT), Differential Item Functioning (DIF), or similar measurement methods.
- Experience in career assessment, personality assessment, educational measurement, organizational psychology, or career development projects.
- Working knowledge of Python, R, SQL, or data-analysis tools.
- Experience with AI-assisted research workflows and the ability to judge the boundaries, hallucinations, and verifiability of model outputs.

### What this role is not

- This is not a clinical diagnosis, counseling, therapy, or medical judgment role.
- This is not a content role that turns psychology terms into entertainment labels.
- This is not a role that only writes explanations without participating in data structure and model-boundary discussions.
- This is not a role where AI replaces research judgment; AI tools are used only to assist organization, verification, and workflow acceleration.

### Suggested application materials

Please send your resume, links to papers or formal research output, and a short description of your research direction. If you have examples related to scale research, data analysis, item design, norm calibration, career assessment, or model interpretation, please include them.

### 2. Visual & Brand UI Designer

**Compensation**: 10K-20K RMB / month, pre-tax, with statutory social insurance and housing fund under the applicable employment arrangement  
**Work mode**: Remote supported  
**Time commitment**: 3-5 days per week  
**Status**: Rolling opening; the role will be updated periodically based on team needs

### Who this is for

This role is for someone who can work across brand systems, website UI, content visuals, and social media presentation. FermatMind is not an entertainment quiz site. Our visual system should feel clear, credible, young, restrained, and technical, rather than relying on exaggerated decoration.

You will need to build a consistent design language across brand positioning, page structure, result-page visuals, video covers, and social media assets.

### What you will own

- Upgrade FermatMind’s brand UI positioning and visual system.
- Design UI for website pages, including homepage, assessment pages, result pages, career pages, article pages, topic pages, trust pages, and campaign landing pages.
- Build visual expressions for assessment results, personality dimensions, career interests, and data explanations.
- Design Bilibili short-video covers, WeChat official-account visuals, social media covers, and content templates.
- Work with engineering to turn design rules into reusable interface structures and component guidelines.
- Use compliant AI-tooling support provided by the team for design research, visual exploration, cover concepts, and content production collaboration.

### What we expect

- A portfolio covering website UI, brand visuals, product interfaces, or content visuals.
- Understanding of information hierarchy, layout rhythm, page conversion paths, and responsive design.
- Ability to build around industrial minimalism, geometric lines, data-dot matrices, clear information hierarchy, and restrained technical clarity.
- Ability to design short-video covers, WeChat visual assets, and social media cover systems.
- Ability to collaborate with frontend engineering and understand basic components, UI states, and responsive constraints.

### Nice to have

- Familiarity with Figma, Adobe tools, AI-assisted design tools, or motion/video-cover workflows.
- Basic understanding of Tailwind CSS, Next.js page structures, or design-to-frontend delivery.
- Experience designing for data products, knowledge products, SaaS, education products, career products, or assessment products.
- A stable design-system method, rather than relying only on one-off visual impact.

### What this role is not

- This is not a role for 3D cute visuals, childish mascots, heavy hand-drawn illustration, or overly entertainment-driven design.
- This is not a role that only produces posters without participating in website experience or product structure.
- This is not a role that only pursues good-looking visuals while ignoring user comprehension, information hierarchy, and long-term brand consistency.
- This is not a template-copying role.

### Suggested application materials

Please send your resume and portfolio. Your portfolio may include website UI, brand systems, mobile pages, video covers, social media visuals, data visualization, or content-packaging examples. Please explain your role and design tradeoffs in each case where possible.

### 3. Technical Partner & CTO

**Compensation**: 30K-50K RMB / month, pre-tax, with statutory social insurance and housing fund under the applicable employment arrangement  
**Work mode**: Full-time preferred; part-time transition accepted for up to six months, with at least 3 working days per week during the transition  
**Status**: Rolling opening; the role will be updated periodically based on team needs

### Partnership structure

This is not an ordinary outsourcing role or a short-term technical advisory slot. We are looking for someone who can take long-term responsibility for the technical system, engineering discipline, and iteration rhythm.

Competitive full-time or part-time compensation can be discussed. Early-stage equity or partner-track arrangements may be discussed after deeper mutual evaluation and will be subject to formal written agreement. Nothing in this hiring announcement should be interpreted as equity, partner status, or long-term incentive being granted automatically.

### Who this is for

This role is for someone who recognizes FermatMind’s value system and is willing to turn a live, evolving project that includes assessments, content, career surfaces, commercial flows, SEO, release workflows, and data systems into a maintainable long-term engineering system.

We want someone with the technical-leadership capacity required by a long-running assessment platform: someone who can read code, improve performance, control risk, build SOPs, standardize releases, manage technical debt, and help the team work better.

### What you will own

- Stabilize the Laravel 12 backend middle platform, including APIs, data structures, cache, queues, permissions, releases, and runtime boundaries.
- Govern Next.js static fallback, SSR/ISR, metadata, canonical, noindex, cache, and runtime stability.
- Take over and continuously improve the Stage 3 Release Train, including readiness, CI, deployment, smoke checks, rollback logic, and sidecar issue workflows.
- Build the project iteration SOP: task splitting, PR review, testing standards, release standards, rollback standards, and runtime validation.
- Manage testing, CI/CD, deployment, observability, performance, security, code quality, and technical debt.
- Identify hardcoded behavior, hidden fallback paths, fragile scripts, unobserved failure paths, and accumulated architectural risk.
- Work with the founder, content, research, and design teammates to make product iteration repeatable, traceable, and auditable.

### What we expect

- Real architecture experience with Laravel / PHP backend middle-platforms or similarly complex business systems.
- Next.js / React engineering experience and understanding of SSR, ISR, static fallback, metadata, SEO, and runtime behavior.
- Familiarity with CI/CD, GitHub Actions, deployment automation, queues, cache, logs, monitoring, and production debugging.
- Ability to establish SOPs rather than relying only on personal firefighting.
- Risk awareness around privacy, commercial flows, result pages, report pages, search exposure, SEO/GEO, and content publishing.
- Strong alignment with FermatMind’s direction: using science, data, and structured tools to help young people build self-understanding and career exploration clarity.

### Nice to have

- Technical leadership experience in long-running content platforms, assessment platforms, SaaS, education products, or data products.
- Laravel + Next.js decoupled architecture experience.
- Experience with Release Train, blue/green deployment, rollback, smoke testing, or multi-environment release governance.
- Experience with security review, performance work, observability, automated testing, and architecture documentation.
- Ability to work with AI coding tools without outsourcing engineering judgment to AI.

### What this role is not

- This is not an ordinary outsourcing development role.
- This is not a role for fixing a few isolated bugs without owning long-term architecture outcomes.
- This is not a role where hardcoding, skipped tests, or bypassing release processes are acceptable.
- This is not a role where equity, partner status, or long-term incentives are granted automatically. Any long-term incentive requires deeper mutual evaluation and formal written agreement.

### Suggested application materials

Please send your resume, GitHub or code repository, architecture examples, technical-debt cleanup examples, or a description of deployment, CI/CD, monitoring, performance, security, or code-quality systems you have led.

## How to Apply

Please send application materials to:

**fuwei8868@outlook.com**

There is no fixed email subject format, but we recommend including the role name, your name or preferred name, and the project keyword you most want us to notice. Please include concrete project examples rather than only a general self-introduction.

## Applicant Privacy

All resumes and application materials will be used only for this team selection process. FermatMind does not connect applications to an automated ATS system and does not sell, publish, or share applicant materials with unrelated third parties.

At the first-contact stage, please do not submit ID numbers, bank details, full home addresses, health information, or other unrelated sensitive personal information.

## Fair Review

FermatMind reviews applications primarily based on role-relevant ability, work samples, research output, project experience, communication quality, and willingness to engage deeply with the work. For current students, recent graduates, or non-traditional candidates with strong AI-tooling ability, strong project execution, or high-quality work samples, collaboration terms and related benefits may be discussed case by case.

title: Privacy Policy
summary: This privacy baseline explains what data FermatMind may process, why it is processed, and how users can manage privacy requests.
contentMd:
## Information we process

FermatMind may process account information, assessment responses, generated reports, order records, device and log data, and support messages when users interact with the service.

## How we use information

We use information to provide assessments and reports, maintain product reliability, process purchases, prevent abuse, improve user experience, and meet legal or operational obligations.

## User choices

Users may request access, correction, deletion, or other privacy support through the available support channels. Some records may need to be retained when required for security, accounting, dispute handling, or legal compliance.

## Contact

For privacy questions, contact FermatMind through the support channel listed on the site.

title: Terms of Use
summary: These terms describe the baseline rules for using FermatMind products, reports, content, and paid services.
contentMd:
## Use of service

FermatMind provides assessment, interpretation, career exploration, and growth support products. Users are responsible for using the service lawfully and for keeping account access secure.

## Assessment boundaries

Reports and recommendations are informational tools. They are not medical diagnosis, therapy, emergency support, hiring authority, admission authority, or a substitute for qualified professional judgment.

## Paid services

Some features may require payment. Product access, refunds, and support handling follow the policies presented at the time of purchase and in related policy pages.

## Changes

We may update products, policies, or terms as the service evolves. Material updates should be reflected in the published content page metadata.

title: Policies Overview
summary: This page organizes FermatMind’s main policy, privacy, boundary, and support resources. It is a directory page that helps users find information about data handling, platform use, assessment boundaries, results privacy, public-benefit giving boundaries, and support channels.
contentMd:
This page organizes FermatMind’s main policy, privacy, boundary, and support resources. It is a directory page that helps users find information about data handling, platform use, assessment boundaries, results privacy, public-benefit giving boundaries, and support channels.

This page does not replace the full policy pages. Specific terms, data handling rules, and service boundaries should be read from the corresponding policy pages.

## Privacy and Data Handling

Read: [Privacy Policy](/en/privacy)

The Privacy Policy explains how FermatMind handles user data in product use, assessment flows, result lookup, support, and basic analytics contexts. It also describes data-use purposes, retention, user rights, and Cookie / analytics-related information.

FermatMind aims to limit unnecessary data handling. This page provides a high-level directory only; specific data handling rules should be read from the Privacy Policy itself.

## Terms of Service

Read: [Terms of Service](/en/terms)

The Terms of Service explain the basic rules for using FermatMind’s website, assessments, reports, content pages, and related tools. They also cover paid services when applicable, report use, user responsibilities, content boundaries, and service limitations.

Assessment results, reports, and career guidance should be understood as structured self-exploration and decision-support materials, not as promises about life outcomes, career outcomes, income, employment, education, or similar results.

## Assessment Method and Boundaries

Read: [Assessment Method and Boundaries](/en/method-boundaries)

FermatMind focuses on personality, career interests, cognitive tendencies, and structured decision support. Assessment outputs can help users organize questions, identify tendencies, compare options, and prepare discussion material.

These outputs are intended for self-understanding and communication support. They should not be used alone in contexts that require professional review, formal institutional judgment, or high-impact consequences.

## Results and Report Privacy

Read: [Privacy Policy](/en/privacy) and [Assessment Method and Boundaries](/en/method-boundaries)

Assessment results, report lookup, and related private information are not intended to be public indexable pages. Result recovery and credential verification tools are isolated private utility paths, not public content directory entries.

Users should avoid posting personal result identifiers, order information, lookup information, or other identifiable private records on public pages, social platforms, or third-party sites.

## Cookie / Consent / Tracking

FermatMind is not adding a standalone Cookie / Consent / Tracking page in this PR. Related information should continue to be handled through the Cookie, analytics, and user-consent sections of the Privacy Policy.

If a standalone Cookie or tracking policy page is needed later, it should be prepared as a separate content and routing decision, with its own visibility, indexing, and content boundaries. It should not be improvised through this policies overview page.

## Public-Benefit Giving Boundary

Read: [Public-Benefit Giving Plan](/en/foundation)

FermatMind’s public-benefit giving plan is a corporate responsibility action initiated, operated, and recorded by FermatMind. It belongs to FermatMind’s own public explanation system and does not change FermatMind’s business identity, product boundaries, assessment methods, or user data handling rules.

The plan does not collect money from users and does not describe product use as a personal donation action. Giving-related records should be read from the official FermatMind page and should remain separated from assessment logic, user data, and privacy boundaries.

## Contact and Support

Visit:

- [Support](/en/support)
- [FAQ](/en/help/faq)
- [Contact](/en/help/contact)

If you have questions about privacy, terms, assessment boundaries, report lookup, public-benefit giving boundaries, or content accuracy, use the support and contact pages. At the first-contact stage, please do not submit ID numbers, bank details, full home addresses, health information, or unrelated sensitive personal information.

title: Assessment Method and Boundaries
summary: How to use FermatMind assessment results responsibly, what they can support, and where their limits begin.
contentMd:
## What FermatMind assessments are

FermatMind assessments are structured self-understanding tools. They collect responses, organize them through defined scoring or interpretation models, and turn the result into language that can help people reflect on personality patterns, interests, work preferences, and growth questions.

A result is best read as a working hypothesis about tendencies in a specific context. It is not a permanent identity, a full biography, or a complete measure of a person's ability, character, health, future, or worth.

## What results can support

Assessment results can help with self-observation, communication, career exploration, learning plans, team conversations, and personal review. They can make patterns easier to name, compare, and discuss.

Results are most useful when combined with real-world evidence: past choices, work samples, feedback from trusted people, repeated behavior over time, and the user's current constraints. They should help users ask better questions and plan next steps, not remove judgment from the user.

## What results cannot decide

FermatMind results do not guarantee school admission, employment, promotion, career success, relationship outcomes, income, health outcomes, or any other high-stakes result. They should not be used as the sole basis for hiring, firing, promotion, compensation, admission, financial decisions, medical decisions, legal decisions, or disciplinary action.

Scores, types, labels, and recommended paths can be incomplete or wrong for a particular person. Human development is affected by environment, opportunity, training, health, relationships, incentives, and time. A report cannot capture all of those conditions.

## Medical and high-stakes limits

FermatMind is not a medical diagnosis, psychological diagnosis, therapy service, crisis service, emergency service, or substitute for qualified professional care. Assessment output should not be treated as a diagnosis of depression, anxiety, ADHD, personality disorder, neurodevelopmental condition, or any other health condition.

If a user is in acute distress, has thoughts of self-harm, experiences serious symptoms, or faces a safety risk, they should seek help from local emergency services, licensed clinicians, trusted family members, or other qualified support instead of relying on an online assessment.

## Data and privacy boundaries

Assessment responses, reports, orders, support messages, device logs, and account information may be processed to provide the service, keep it reliable, handle orders, respond to support requests, protect security, and improve product quality. Privacy handling follows the published Privacy Policy and the notices shown in product flows.

Users should avoid entering unnecessary sensitive information into free-text fields. Organizations should not request or expose individual results unless the purpose, authorization, visibility, retention, appeal path, and privacy obligations are clear.

## How to use results responsibly

Use results as structured input for reflection. Start with one or two claims that feel useful, compare them with real behavior, and test a small next step for a defined period. Keep what is useful, challenge what does not fit, and review the result again when circumstances change.

For education, career, clinical, legal, financial, or employment decisions, combine any assessment insight with qualified human judgment, relevant professional standards, direct evidence, and the user's own goals. The assessment is a tool for thinking, not an authority that decides for the user.
